question: If I provision a Cache-T1 instance for exactly 2 hours and transfer 100GB of data entirely within the same CloudScale region, what is my exact total cost?
expected_answer: Your total cost is $0.03. The Cache-T1 instance costs $0.015/hour (2 hours = $0.03), and data transfer within the same CloudScale region is completely free.
reference_context: CloudScale Managed Redis databases are priced hourly based on the memory capacity and replication configuration: - Cache-T1 (1GB RAM, Single Node): $0.015/hour (~$11/month)... Network ingress is free, but egress to the public internet is billed at $0.08 per GB. Data transfer within the same CloudScale region is free of charge.

question: I am a 'Developer' in my organization. Can I invite a new backend engineer as a 'Viewer' to check logs, or do I need my 'Admin' to do it?
expected_answer: You need an Admin or Owner to do it. Developers can manage applications and databases, but they cannot edit organization settings or member roles, nor can they invite new members.
reference_context: 5. Select a Role: - Owner: Full access, including billing and organization deletion. - Admin: Can manage all resources, settings, and invites, but cannot view billing or delete the organization. - Developer: Can manage applications, databases, and services, but cannot edit organization settings or member roles. - Viewer: Read-only access to all services and logs.

question: How do I configure a custom domain name using the CloudScale Free Tier?
expected_answer: You cannot use a custom domain name on the Free Tier. Custom domains require upgrading to at least the Pro tier. The Free Tier only provides a standard shared CloudScale subdomain.
reference_context: The Free Tier includes 1 app, shared compute, and a cloudscale.io subdomain. To use a custom domain name, SSL certificates, or Auto-Scaling, you must upgrade your organization to the Pro Tier or higher.

question: My single-node PostgreSQL database failed. Will it automatically failover to a replica with zero downtime?
expected_answer: No. Single-node dedicated databases do not automatically failover because there is no replica. CloudScale will attempt crash recovery on a replacement node, which incurs downtime. For zero-downtime failover, you must explicitly provision an HA (High Availability) replica.
reference_context: CloudScale Managed PostgreSQL takes automated daily backups retained for 7 days. If your database node fails, CloudScale will automatically attempt crash recovery on a replacement node. However, this incurs downtime. For zero-downtime failover, you must provision an HA (High Availability) replica. Failover to an HA replica is automatic and typically resolves in under 10 seconds.

question: Is public ingress (incoming traffic) billed at the standard $0.08 per GB rate like egress?
expected_answer: No, network ingress (incoming data) is completely free of charge. Only public egress (outgoing data to the public internet) is billed at $0.08 per GB.
reference_context: Network ingress is free, but egress to the public internet is billed at $0.08 per GB. Data transfer within the same CloudScale region is free of charge.
